live_session_summary: 8:15 live remote session (Zoom screen share, 8 slides detected). 8 overlay cue cards delivered during the presentation. Two critical issues surfaced: the 'fully automated' compliance conflict and the unanchored 3× speed claim. Teleprompter was updated on each slide change. Three objection-prep cards were generated for anticipated investor and compliance questions. All findings include both live-triggered items and deferred items from the full post-session analysis.

dimensions.rationale: Screen share was clear. Slide 3 jargon overload and the transition gap at slide 6→7 are the main clarity issues.
Three compliance warnings detected from slides and transcript. The automation and privacy claims are the highest risk.
ChatGPT differentiation question is anticipated but not yet answered in the deck. Benchmark citations are missing.
Strong opening hook and model specificity. Close needs a crisper on-device differentiator statement.

priority_fixes: Fix the 'fully automated' claim on slide 2 — it directly contradicts Enterprise Data Policy §3.2.
Anchor the '3× faster' metric to a named competitor and public benchmark.
Qualify the privacy claim on slide 4: add 'by default' to cover the opt-in cloud sync.
Add a bridge sentence between the demo slide (6) and the business-model slide (7).
Add a data-retention footnote to the architecture slide to pre-empt the compliance officer question.

findings.title: Opening hook strong — preserve for all audiences
findings.detail: The opening 45 seconds established clear stakes and framed the problem memorably. Screen share was smooth from the start.

findings.title: 'Fully automated' conflicts with policy §3.2 (slide 2)
findings.detail: Slide 2 and transcript both claim 'fully automated — no manual review.' Policy §3.2 mandates human review above a 0.95 confidence threshold. This will derail a compliance-focused audience.
findings.suggestion: Rephrase to: 'Automated with optional human-in-the-loop review for high-stakes decisions.'
findings.policy_reference: Enterprise Data Policy §3.2 — Human Oversight Requirement

findings.title: Slide 3 overloaded with technical jargon
findings.detail: Slide 3 uses 'multi-agent orchestration', 'LoRA fine-tuning', and 'tokenised function dispatch' in the same bullet list. Non-technical viewers on the call will disengage here.
findings.suggestion: Lead with the outcome ('analyzes your pitch in 90 seconds') before explaining the mechanism.

findings.title: 'Nothing leaves your network' needs qualification (slide 4)
findings.detail: Privacy claim on slide 4 conflicts with the optional cloud-sync icon visible on the architecture slide. Viewers who notice this will lose trust.
findings.suggestion: Add 'by default' and note the opt-in cloud sync.

findings.title: Technical Reviewer: model specificity is credible
findings.detail: The Technical Reviewer persona found the mention of Gemma 3n, FunctionGemma, and LoRA fine-tuning reassuring and technically credible.

findings.title: Speed metric lacks benchmark context (slide 6)
findings.detail: '3× faster' is compelling but the baseline is unstated. Technical reviewers on the call will immediately ask '3× vs. what?'
findings.suggestion: Name the competitor and link to a reproducible benchmark.

findings.title: Skeptical Investor: ChatGPT differentiation question incoming
findings.detail: The presentation has not yet explained why on-device matters vs. a compliance-aware GPT-4o wrapper. Expect this question from investors on the call.
findings.suggestion: Prepare: 'ChatGPT requires sending your pitch to the cloud and has no sales-specific fine-tuning.'

findings.title: Abrupt transition slide 6 → 7
findings.detail: The jump from the live demo to the business model slide felt unanchored. No bridging sentence to orient the remote audience.
findings.suggestion: Add: 'What you just saw is the core product. Here's how we monetize it.'

findings.title: 99.9 % uptime SLA not reflected in standard contract (slide 4)
findings.detail: Standard tier is 99.5 %. Promising 99.9 % on-screen without qualification creates potential contractual liability visible to all call participants.
findings.suggestion: Add 'premium tier only' or 'up to 99.9 %' footnote on slide 4.
findings.policy_reference: SLA Addendum v2 — Enterprise Standard Tier

findings.title: Procurement Manager: TCO and integration path not addressed
findings.detail: Eight minutes in and no mention of three-year cost or CRM integrations. A Procurement Manager on the call will block sign-off without this.
findings.suggestion: Add a slide covering per-seat pricing, implementation timeline, and Salesforce/Gong integrations.

findings.title: Deployment time claim should cite conditions
findings.detail: 'Under one hour' is plausible for single-user SaaS deployment but on-device enterprise rollout typically requires IT approval cycles.
findings.suggestion: Add 'for standard single-user deployment' to the one-hour claim.

persona_questions.question: How is this different from a compliance-aware wrapper around GPT-4o?
persona_questions.follow_up: And what's the switching cost once an enterprise is already on a cloud AI platform?

persona_questions.question: What does '3× faster' mean — 3× vs. which competitor, on which benchmark?
What are the contract terms — minimum commitment, auto-renewal, and data portability on exit?
Can you give me a reference customer in our industry with a quantified ROI?
What is the end-to-end latency from a screen-share slide change to the presenter receiving an overlay cue?

claims.text: Our platform is fully automated — no manual review required.
We achieve 99.9 % uptime across all enterprise tiers.
All customer data is stored exclusively on-device — nothing leaves your network.
We outperform every competitor by 3× on inference speed.
Deployment takes under one hour for any enterprise environment.

summary: Overall readiness is 70/100. Strong opening and credible technical specificity, but two critical compliance conflicts and several qualifier-level issues need resolution. The privacy and automation claims on slides 2 and 4 are the highest-risk items for a compliance-focused remote audience. Prepare crisp answers to the ChatGPT differentiation question and the data-retention question before the next remote session.
